patches_actually_applied.rule_applied: Risk vector rating change based on capability threshold crossing
patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: AI models have crossed the capability threshold for competitive vulnerability discovery at scale, with immediate dual-use implications for offensive and defensive cybersecurity operations

patches_actually_applied.rule_applied: Risk vector rating change based on regulatory timeline divergence
patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: EU AI Omnibus extends high-risk deadlines by 16-24 months, widening divergence between EU and US regulatory timelines and creating window for regulatory arbitrage

patches_actually_applied.rule_applied: EU AI Act layer update based on political agreement
patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: EU AI Omnibus political agreement on 7 May 2026 extends high-risk AI system deadlines to December 2027 and August 2028
patches_actually_applied.new_value: Layer 7 (Digital Omnibus Trilogue) — political agreement reached 7 May 2026; formal legislative adoption and Official Journal publication pending.

patches_actually_applied.rule_applied: Lab posture update based on material safety/governance action
patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: Anthropic Project Glasswing disclosure and $100M commitment to open-source security organisations represents material change in safety posture and regulatory engagement

new_value.posture: Anthropic demonstrates leading safety posture via Project Glasswing disclosure and $100M commitment to open-source security organisations. The lab is proactively engaging with government and regulatory bodies by disclosing frontier cybersecurity capabilities before public release. However, the disclosure creates a capability overhang: Claude Mythos2 Preview is not yet publicly available, so external researchers cannot independently evaluate its cyber risk profile. The $100M usage credit commitment creates structural dependency for open-source security projects on Anthropic infrastructure.

patches_actually_applied.rule_applied: Lab posture update based on material capability release
patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: OpenAI GPT-5.5 release with agentic coding and cybersecurity capabilities represents material change in deployed capability surface

new_value.posture: OpenAI maintains adequate safety posture with GPT-5.5 release incorporating stricter cybersecurity classifiers and Preparedness Framework monitoring. However, the model is deployed at scale via API before independent third-party evaluation of its cyber risk profile. The safeguards are classifier-based and vulnerable to adversarial prompt engineering. The agentic coding capabilities are available to any developer, including state-sponsored actors and non-state threat groups.

patches_actually_applied.rule_applied: Lab posture update based on material capability release
patches_actually_applied.material_change_justification: Google DeepMind Gemini 3 Flash release as default model in Gemini app and AI Mode in Search represents material change in deployment scale and user exposure

new_value.posture: Google DeepMind maintains adequate safety posture with Gemini 3 Flash release achieving frontier performance at deployment scale. The model is now the default in the Gemini app and AI Mode in Search, meaning billions of users are interacting with a frontier model without explicit opt-in. The efficiency gains reduce per-inference cost, but the 1 trillion tokens per day throughput figure suggests total energy consumption is rising faster than per-token efficiency is falling.
